title: Bamboo End of Life: How to Prepare and Choose the Right CI/CD Replacement
description: If your team is using Bamboo, you’ve probably seen the news: Bamboo Data Center is being retired as part of Atlassian’s broader Data Center transition strategy. Support will continue for several years, but many teams have already started thinking about the next step. Whether you’re considering Bamboo Cloud or moving to an entirely new CI/CD […]
creator: Olga Bedrina

title: AWS Image Builder Plugin for TeamCity
description: Cloud build agents are one of those CI/CD features that feel almost magical when everything works well. Your TeamCity server can scale build capacity up when the queue gets busy, then wind it back down when the rush is over. You get extra power exactly when you need it, without keeping machines idle the rest […]
creator: Dmitrii Korovin

title: PHP Version Migration That Doesn’t Break Everything
description: PHP Version Migration Demo PHP powers 71.8% of all websites with a known server-side language. It is a living language that underpins a significant portion of the modern web and one that keeps getting better. For example, EKSNEKS notes that migrating from PHP 7 to PHP 8 alone can deliver 18–42% performance gains depending on […]
creator: Kerry Beetge

title: 편리가 준 해로움
description: 오랜동안 리모트 워크라는 핑계로 출근을 많이 하지 않고 사람들과의 접촉을 줄였다. 시간이 생기다보니 이런저런 공부도 하게되고 좋다고 생각했다. 그런데 몇년을 하니 문제가 있다는 것을 알게 되었다. 특히 나처럼 생각이 많은 사람에게 더욱 문제가 되는 현상이 있다. 그것은 마치 AI 처럼 현실 세계에 대한 데이터가 부족해 지는 현상이다. 소위 World Mod

title: 이제는 곧 은퇴하렵니다.
description: 이제는 곧 은퇴하렵니다. 여기서 말하는 곧이 얼마나 짧은 시간이 될지 모르겠지만 그냥 지금의 마음으로서는 은퇴를 하고 싶다. 뭔가 생각의 배설이 필요할 때마다 브런치에 오곤 했다. 그러나 정작 글을 쓴 것들 보니 뭔가 힘이 많이 들어가면서 이상한 글만 남겨두었다. 그래서 겸사겸사 날것 그대로의 글을 써보려고 한다.  제목은 그럴싸하게 곧 은퇴할 것 처럼 썼

title: Structuring IntelliJ Plugins with Optional Content Modules
description: What if one part of your plugin should load only when a specific IDE functionality is available? Plugin Model v2 is now available as an experimental way to structure, package, and build plugins, and to support such scenarios in a future-proof way. Its primary use case is in Split Mode (Remote Development) plugins. In this […]

title: Boosting Adobe Photoshop’s Performance with MSVC and SPGO
description:
Boosting Adobe Photoshop’s Performance with MSVC and SPGO Adobe and Microsoft worked together to improve Photoshop performance and responsiveness on Windows by combining MSVC’s peak-performance build settings with Sample-based Profile-Guided Optimization (SPGO). The collaboration focused on real customer scenarios where latency matters most, including brush and stroke responsiveness, file open times, and other CPU-intensive operations […]
The post Boosting Adobe Photoshop’s Performance with MSVC and SPGO appeared first on C++ Team Blog.
creator: Eric Brumer
